Dept. 514. Judge: Cynthia Ming-Mei Lee. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Nichole M. Rodich, CSR #11604. Telephone: 415-551-0616.

Michael Espinosa, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs James Fletcher, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Jackson Fletcher, Deceased; and Morie Frank, as Wrongful Death Heirs of Jackson Fletcher, Deceased.
No appearance by defaulted defendant City Builders Supply.
Complaint was filed on August 30, 2013.
First Amended Complaint was filed on November 25, 2104.
Default was entered against defendant on March 18, 2015.
Statement of Damages was filed and served on March 18, 2015.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on January 8, 2018.

Economic Damages: $255,751.82.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Discovery and motion dates and deadlines remain closed as to all other parties. Century made an affirmative showing of good cause requiring the continuance, as required by CRC 3.1332. APPEARANCES: Kimberly Chu, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP 415/898-1555 for pltfs & moving parties; Laura Przetak, Esq. of Berry & Berry 510/250-0425 Designated Defense Counsel; (Refer to Sign-in Sheet for all other appearances).

RULING: Hearing held. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Trial is May 5, 2014 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day for hearing summary motions is April 30, 2014. The time to respond to written discovery is shortened to 20 days "for discovery propounded from today on, only." Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than March 7, 2014. Responses to the demand are due no later than March 14, 2014. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on April 24, 2014. Plaintiff is ordered to provide to the defense all necessary documents in order for the defense to obtain plaintiff's father's social security and employment records by January 10, 2014. Fact discovery cut-off date is April 18, 2014. Expert discovery cut-off date is May 2, 2014. No later than January 21, 2014 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than January 21, 2014 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Before any motions in limine are filed with the court the defendants are ordered to meet and confer on all motions in limine in order to avoid frivolous and duplicative hearings. If the parties do not meet and confer on the motions in limine the court will take appropriate actions. Each side is limited to a total of five motions in limine not to exceed five pages per motion. If a particular party requires additional motions in limine, this request shall be made to the assigned trial judge. Parties are ordered to meet and confer on deposition designations on the same day the case is assigned to a trial department. Deposition designations shall be submitted to the trial judge on the third day after assignment. Further case management conference on March 26, 2014 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on May 5, 2014 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by May 1, 2014 at 2:00p.m. "The MILs will be filed on the day of the trial assignment." Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kim Avery of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5074 (510/451-1580) (503/TLJ)
